summary: This is a very brief news post where Joel Spolsky shares a link to an interview he did with Scott Rosenberg for Salon magazine. The post consists of a single sentence and a pull quote from the article that humorously connects software writing to Yiddish humor. It serves primarily as a pointer to external content rather than containing substantive argument or analysis.
key_points: Joel was interviewed by Scott Rosenberg for Salon, a major online publication
The interview positioned Joel as a unique voice connecting software commentary with humor
By 2004, Joel on Software had gained enough prominence to warrant mainstream media coverage
money_quotes: The connection between software and Yiddish humor may not have been evident until Joel Spolsky began writing his Joel on Software essays and blog in 2000.
key_insight: By 2004, Joel on Software had achieved enough cultural influence to attract coverage from mainstream publications like Salon, recognized for its distinctive blend of technical insight and humor.
